agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"Multi-step distributed operation with zero error handling guarantees orphaned state on any partial failure"},"critical_findings":[{"title":"Partial failure leaves irrecoverable orphaned state","risk":"If any step after `reserve()` fails, the system enters an inconsistent state with no rollback. A reserved courier is never released, an order may show ASSIGNED with no dispatch sent, or a courier dispatches with no customer notification. Each failure point produces a different broken state that requires manual intervention.","severity":"Critical","location":"assignAndDispatch steps 1-4 (entire function)","reproduction":"1. Call assignAndDispatch. 2. courierService.reserve succeeds (courier is now locked/reserved). 3. orderService.updateStatus throws (DB timeout, constraint violation). Result: courier is reserved but order is not updated — courier is stuck in limbo, unavailable for other orders, and no one knows.","fix":"Wrap in try/catch with compensating actions (saga pattern). On failure after step 1, call courierService.release(courierId). On failure after step 2, also revert order status. Alternatively, use an outbox/state-machine pattern where each step is idempotent and retryable.","time_to_fix":"1-2 hours","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Order assignment reliability, courier availability correctness"},{"title":"No idempotency — retry causes double-reserve and double-notify","risk":"If a caller retries assignAndDispatch after a transient failure (or a queue redelivers the message), reserve() is called again, potentially locking a second courier for the same order. Notifications are also sent multiple times.","severity":"Critical","location":"courierService.reserve(orderId) — step 1","reproduction":"1. First call: reserve succeeds, updateStatus times out. 2. Caller retries: reserve is called again with same orderId, reserving a second courier. First courier remains orphaned.","fix":"Add idempotency key or check: before reserving, verify the order doesn't already have a reserved courier. Make reserve() idempotent on orderId (return existing reservation if one exists). Add idempotency tokens to notification calls.","time_to_fix":"1-2 hours","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Courier availability, duplicate notification prevention"},{"title":"Sequential await chain creates cascading latency and a wide failure window","risk":"Four sequential network calls means the window for partial failure is maximized. If notificationService is slow or down, the entire operation blocks despite the core business logic (reserve + update) being complete. Customer-facing and courier-facing notifications have different criticality but are treated identically.","severity":"High","location":"Steps 3-4: notificationService.sendDispatchAlert / sendCustomerAlert","reproduction":"1. Steps 1-2 succeed in 50ms. 2. sendDispatchAlert hangs for 30s then times out. 3. Customer never gets notified. 4. If no timeout is configured, function hangs indefinitely.","fix":"Separate core transactional work (reserve + update) from side-effects (notifications). Fire notifications asynchronously via an event/queue after the transaction commits. At minimum, wrap steps 3-4 in independent try/catch blocks so one notification failure doesn't block the other, and add timeouts.","time_to_fix":"30 minutes for try/catch separation, 2-4 hours for event-driven refactor","acceptance_criteria_affected":"End-to-end latency, notification reliability"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No timeout or circuit breaker on external service calls","description":"Each await has no timeout. A hung courierService or notificationService will block the caller indefinitely, potentially exhausting connection pools or worker threads upstream.","location":"All four await calls"},{"title":"No logging or observability","description":"When a partial failure occurs, there is no trace of which step failed or what state was left behind. Debugging orphaned couriers in production will be extremely difficult without structured logging at each step.","location":"Entire function"},{"title":"No return value for caller to track outcome","description":"Function returns void — the caller has no way to know which courier was assigned or whether notifications succeeded. This makes retries and monitoring harder.","location":"Function signature"}],"suggestions":["Implement a saga/orchestrator pattern with explicit compensating transactions for each step","Add structured logging with a correlation ID (orderId) at each step boundary","Return a result object with courierId and step completion status","Add a dead-letter mechanism for failed notifications so they can be retried independently","Consider making steps 3 and 4 parallel via Promise.allSettled since they are independent of each other"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"Any order assignment under real traffic will eventually hit a partial failure, leaving orphaned couriers and inconsistent order states that require manual database intervention","rollback_capability":"No rollback mechanism exists — this is the core problem","backward_compatibility":"N/A — new function","monitoring_coverage":"Zero observability — no logs, no metrics, no alerting on partial failures","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":false,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":[]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Do not deploy without compensating transaction logic. If already deployed, add manual runbook to query for reserved couriers with no matching ASSIGNED order and release them."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Partial failure orphaned state is guaranteed to occur in production","Retry-safety is completely absent","Notification failures block the entire flow"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether courierService.reserve has its own internal idempotency (unlikely but possible)","Whether there is an external retry/queue mechanism wrapping this function"],"assumptions":["Each service call is a separate network/RPC call with no shared transaction","The caller may retry on failure","No external saga orchestrator wraps this function"]}}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this courier assignment flow for critical issues.

The function has a clear multi-step process with no error handling, no rollback, and no idempotency. Let me identify the top risks.
